title: erigeron acris subsp. lalehzaricus
intro: Erigeron acris subsp. lalehzaricus is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Erigeron acris species, commonly known as the tall fleabane or tall daisy. This plant is a perennial herb native to Iran, characterized by its white to pinkish flowers and hairy stems. The medicinal properties of Erigeron acris subsp. lalehzaricus are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can be achieved through herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, or tinctures.
